mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and declining consumer confidence, which directly translates into lower corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where UNRATE has increased by more than 0.2 percentage points over the prior 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Extremely low federal funds rates, specifically below 0.5%, indicate aggressive monetary easing during a crisis, which eventually stimulates economic activity and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as market confidence gradually returns.
failure_condition: If, during periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, the average 60-day forward return of the S&P 500 is not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous and significant decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and a sharp increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicate a severe contraction in both consumer demand and labor market health, leading to significant negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales falling and unemployment rising in late 2008/early 2009.
failure_condition: If, when RSAFS declines by more than 1% month-over-month and UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points month-over-month, the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp and sustained contraction in industrial production, specifically a 3-month average change below -1.0%, signals a broad-based decline in manufacturing and supply-side activity, which translates into lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon during periods of severe economic contraction.
failure_condition: If, during severe economic contractions (e.g., NBER-defined recession), a 3-month average decline in INDPRO below -1.0% does not precede negative S&P 500 60-day returns in at least 60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: An uptick in housing starts, specifically a 3-month average change greater than 5%, even from historically low levels, indicates a nascent recovery in the housing market and broader economic confidence, which typically precedes positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as demand begins to rebound.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 550k in Dec 2008, historically low.
failure_condition: If a 3-month average increase in HOUST greater than 5% is not followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns in at least 65% of instances during early recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in consumer price inflation, indicated by a 3-month annualized change in CPIAUCSL exceeding 3%, erodes real corporate earnings and consumer purchasing power, leading to immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
regime_hint: stagflationary environment or rising inflation post-recession
failure_condition: If, when CPIAUCSL's 3-month annualized change exceeds 3%, the S&P 500's 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A stabilization or slight increase in non-farm payrolls, specifically when the 3-month average change in PAYEMS turns positive, signals a turning point in the labor market and improved consumer outlook, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If, after a period of job losses, the 3-month average change in PAYEMS turning positive does not lead to a statistically significant positive average 60-day S&P 500 return (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A return to positive GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with persistently low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS below 1.0%) indicates a supportive environment for economic recovery and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns positive AND FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 was negative in Q4 2008, FEDFUNDS very low.
regime_hint: post-recessionary recovery with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: If periods where GDPC1 growth is positive and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0% do not exhibit an average 60-day S&P 500 return significantly higher than the overall average (t-test p < 0.05).
